programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz take on carpenter Mikes 1980 Ford Escort RS2000. After his sons road accident, Mikes family want to repay his selfless devotion and give him the Car SOS treatment.

programme.desc: In Tuscany, Gill and Robin turn an old cantina into a rural hillside retreat. Stacey sets her sights on opening a yoga studio. David and Lanes relocation doesnt go to plan.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This time the competition takes place in and around Crewe and Nantwich. First to host is hairdresser Mushgon Dadfar. Loud, proud and confident, Mushgons got high hopes for her Caribbean themed evening, but what will the guests make of Mushgons choice of zumba for entertainment - always a good idea after a heavy meal.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Second to host is 44-year-old housewife Pip Massey. As a lady who lunches and is quite used to entertaining, Pip has high expectations that all those years of practise will make her `glamorous evening a success.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Landscape gardener and joker of the pack Darren Leech is third to host in and around Crewe and Nantwich. Darren plans to wow his guests with fine food and lashings of his unique entertainment. The menu features smoked haddock and black pudding, and chicken pie; the entertainment includes big laughs at Darrens bottom.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Student Emma Davies is the fourth to host. As the baby of the group, Emma feels she is just the person to inject a bit of fun into the week with her shipwrecked dinner-party theme. Then theres her inventive stuffed pork recipe, a homemade gnocchi starter, but also a musical performance from the host.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The week of dining in and around Crewe and Nantwich is rounded off with hypnotherapist Russell Stubbss `British rock night. Will Russells great British produce be enough to convince his guests that he should win the £1,000?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Maria and Andy love Cyprus because they met on the island 20 years ago, got married there, and are now planning to move there. Jean Johansson shows them what their £140k can buy in Famagusta.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett explores how the Normans developed from a band of marauding Vikings into the formidable warriors who conquered England in 1066. He tells how the Normans established their new province of Normandy - `land of the northmen - in northern France. They went on to build some of the finest churches in Europe and turned into an unstoppable force of Christian knights and warriors.

programme.desc: Baker and dessert-lover Briony May Williams embarks on a journey to discover whether warnings of the imminent demise of the British pudding are overcooked. programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Award-winning comedian Jayde Adams is on hand to help as for one night only the queens form comedy double acts for RuPauls Fringe Festival. But who will have the last laugh? Amandaland star Lucy Punch joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Graham Norton as another queen sashays away.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.sub-title: Sugar and Spice: A British Pudding Adventure
programme.desc: Baker and dessert-lover Briony May Williams embarks on a journey to discover whether warnings of the imminent demise of the British pudding are overcooked. She meets a pudding fan club in the Cotswolds, heads to the Lake District to meet the makers of what may have become the countrys favourite pudding and tests out the sweet tooths of pudding fans of the future to see if theres any hope for this British classic.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.sub-title: Sugar and Spice: A British Pudding Adventure
programme.desc: Baker and dessert-lover Briony May Williams embarks on a journey to discover whether warnings of the imminent demise of the British pudding are overcooked. She meets a pudding fan club in the Cotswolds, heads to the Lake District to meet the makers of what may have become the countrys favourite pudding and tests out the sweet tooths of pudding fans of the future to see if theres any hope for this British classic.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: Back in LIsle-sur-la-Sorgue, a mysterious pair of metal boots catch Jons eye. Meanwhile, Lucy, who literally wrote the book on mid-century chairs, buys with her heart rather than her head.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Cornwalls Tintagel, famed as the legendary birthplace of King Arthur. Ben discovers Tintagel was potentially more important than London a thousand years ago, based on recent evidence unearthed at an archaeological dig overseen by English Heritage. It was found that over 2,000 people were living here which, at the time, which would have made it the largest settlement anywhere of its kind in Britain.

programme.title: Italys Invisible Cities
programme.desc: With the help of 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the watery wonderland of Venice. They uncover how a city built in a swamp became one of the most powerful in Medieval Europe, and dive into its canals to experience how the city remains standing.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and antiques experts Michael Baggott and Kate Bateman visit Dulwich College in London. Michael falls in love with a Chinese censor, Kate is enamoured by a charming collection of bronze animals and Paul makes the short journey to Dulwich Picture Gallery, where he is privileged to see one of the best collections of European old masters in the world.

programme.desc: When a sinister cave is discovered in a remote corner of Iceland, experts uncover a secret underworld tunneling to unknown depths, and using cutting-edge tech, they investigate if this strange place could hold secrets of alien worlds.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In 1997, thousands of people across Phoenix witness a series of strange lights unlike anything theyve ever seen. Could this be one of the largest-scale UFO sightings in history, or is there another explanation for this mysterious phenomenon?

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: One of the holiest and most powerful relics from the Bible has been missing for over 2,000 years. While some believe the Ark of the Covenant was destroyed, many people think its still out there. Could new clues finally tell us what happened?

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In 1930, a New York Supreme Court justice with close ties to Tammany Hall, and future president Franklin Delano Roosevelt, steps out of a steakhouse and disappears. For decades, the case dominates mainstream media and remains unsolved.

programme.title: Holy Marvels With Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: Legendary beasts are believed to roam the earth and the seas, terrorising, killing, and even possessing humans. But are these creatures merely metaphors or is there proof theyve actually been unleashed to wreak havoc?

programme.title: Clash of Ancient Warriors
programme.desc: Battle of Sekigahara: The Battle of Sekigahara saw Tokugawa Ieyasu emerge victorious over his rival Ishida Mitsunari, uniting the country under the Tokugawa shogunate.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Many mysteries of planet Earth are far from understood. How was Devils Tower formed? Or why do some people get struck by lightning multiple times?

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Millions of people around the world claim to have had an encounter with creatures unlike any recognised by mainstream science, such as the Mothman, Chupacabra, and Mongolian Death Worm.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Lost Language, The Beauty Machine And The Disk Of Strangeness
programme.desc: Examine a tablet linked to a lost civilization, a machine that measures beauty, and an ancient Egyptian object.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Aliens may have tried to make contact with Earthlings for millennia, and a message may have been decoded.

programme.title: Unsealed: Alien Files
programme.desc: Experts investigate why so many UFO sightings take place in Los Angeles, determining that the answer may lie underwater.

programme.title: Bloomberg Horizons Middle East and Africa

programme.title: Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe
programme.desc: Live market reports from London. Overnight on Wall Street is early morning in Europe. Markets never sleep, and neither does Bloomberg News. Track investments 24 hours a day, around the clock from around the world.

It looks like Boy might win, until another accident causes Cats whole body to get stuck to his face.

Gavin is instructed to keep watch, though hes a little distracted. Meanwhile, Ricky makes a shiny selfie in homage to Gustav Klimt, an octopus using a candle and a squirty flower! With his flat half empty, can Ricky finally recover his stuff and uncover his mystery fan?

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.title: Armorsaurs

programme.title: Tish Tash
programme.desc: Its Saturday and Tish wants to watch her `Zoo Friends TV show, but Rosa insists she finish her chores.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula have a penny each for the surprise machine in Padgets shop. Sula gets the silver rhino she wanted, so Bing is convinced he will get the alien he wants.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Its a windy day and the Teletubbies collect lots of leaves that have blown off the trees. The leaves go everywhere in Home Dome, so the Noo-noo cleans up.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tees friends Tomo and Lily seem to be the best at everything. Tee wonders what he is the best at?

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea thinks the Veggies wont like it when she covers the supermarket in ice, but instead they have the best snow day ever, so she creates a giant snowball to ruin their fun.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Coco are drawing Gilly a picture. When she and Popsie arrive, Bing goes to say hello, but while he does so Charlie makes a big squiggle on Bings drawing. Gilly shows them a game called `squiggling. You draw a squiggle, and then your partner turns that squiggle into a new drawing. Bing loves this game and turns Charlies squiggle into Popsie.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Bryan the ferry cant shut his engine off, so hes speeding out of control around the lake. How will Mojo and Bo get aboard to take control and steer Bryan to safety?

programme.title: Hey Duggees Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Today at Squirrel Club, Duggee forgets how to woof, Duggly comes to visit, Roly celebrates Potato Time, and Billy teaches everyone the Bugatron dance!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee has made a lovely pie for everyone to share, but slicing a pie five ways is tricky. The Squirrels try to figure out the best to share equally.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Tired of being told what to do, Bluey and Bingo enlist their giant invisible friend to show Mum and Dad whos boss. Now they can do anything they want, until Mum and Dad rebel.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: In the park, Bluey meets a new playmate, Winnie, but when Bluey wants to invite her and her dad home for breakfast, she learns that adults take a little bit longer to make friends.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitchs plans to open a hotel on an unspoilt beach in Thailand go wrong when macaque monkeys steal his Grim-o-freeze gun. It doesnt take long for the trouble to spread across Monkey Bay, so the Go Jetters leap into action to help save the beach.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Tiny Terror
programme.desc: Peters plans to raid Mr McGregors garden are thwarted when he discovers the Shrew has got there first. Its down to the rabbits to find a way to get their garden back.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Exploring the secrets of a mysterious pyramid, the friends find clues in bar charts.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: From A to Z, every `Alphablock has fun discovering the magic of wordplay. Sing along as the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

programme.title: Tiny Wonders
programme.desc: The children are in the meadow. The Nogglins discover a land of hexagons, and everywhere looks the same. Itty leads the way, but she must also find a route back.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster worries about showing people his favourite conch shell when everyone else has unicorn-themed items for their show and tell presentations.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift visit the Fortress of Fun, where Kingsley and Queenie have a fun snail slime race. But who will win, Team King or Team Queen?

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its the marvellous Cabbage and his dazzling super veggie memory skills!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing falls in love with a lost dog he and Flop find in the park and decides he wants to keep her. When the owner turns up, Bing is very upset when it comes to saying goodbye to his new friend.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `lizard, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. Today we see a green lizard, a bright lizard, a long-tailed lizard, a running lizard and a climbing lizard.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: It is Elodies last day in the Shell School and the bugs decide to celebrate by playing some games.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: The Piggies help Reg harvest his delicious banananuts. Wilf eagerly offers to help, but only because he wants to keep them all for himself! How will the Piggies stop him from taking the whole harvest?

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a lady trying to make a picnic bench, fitting planks of wood together. Her dog comes to tell her it is time for a walk and the Twirlywoos start fitting pieces together, but they may be making something completely different.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin is down on the farm with his friends where they meet a chicken, find some eggs and get creative. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble finds a wiggly worm in Grandad Tumbles garden.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies do conga dances over the hills, then they conga into Home Dome to answer the Tubby Phone and do a special conga Tubby Phone Dance. In Tummy Tales, the children have lots of fun as they conga around a soft play centre.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome Zuri, Otis, Theo, Reau, Elijah and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Giovanna has a tambourine inside her bag. All the parents and babies have different noisy objects and toys in their bags too.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee plays I Spy with her friend Athena whilst on a canal boat trip.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie and Triggs sneak and creak their way past mum and dads bedroom in search of a tasty treat, only to discover that they may not be the only ones searching for a midnight feast.

programme.title: Dylans Playtime Adventures
programme.desc: Dylan the pizza delivery pup wants to deliver a pizza fast, but every shortcut is an adventure.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou have a big fight, but they soon find its too hard not to be friends.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: While on a trip to the shops, Bingo and Bluey create issues for their parents when they insist on playing a game of `Kids.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Tino invites everyone to enter his summer exhibition competition but the Squirrels are not sure what to create. Duggee suggest to paint something they know and love.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers arrive on a planet where everything is made of wool. They have fun playing together, but their plans change when they have to go in search of Ninas missing doll.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: When Timmys glove puppet goes missing, he creates chaos as he hunts for it, digging up the sandpit, searching vehicles and frisking his classmates, but theres no sign of it.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.sub-title: Iona and Elba: Elbas Birthday Party
programme.desc: Its Elbas birthday and sister Iona is planning a very special tea party for her. Iona and Elba decorate the bedroom ready for the big party, and there are lots of balloons and games to enjoy.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Vinnies family is English and they come from a city called Bristol, which is located in the west of England. Bristol is famous for hot-air balloons and pirates! Vinnie makes his tarts by mixing the leaves from parsley and thyme into cream cheese.

programme.title: Ferne and Rorys Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Xiangfeng wants to know all about amazing ants. Ferne visits a leaf cutter ant colony at the zoo, while Rory and his son Sandy head into the forest, looking for wood ants. Plus, find out how ants play an important role in the growing of plants.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Rayane, Karim and Luca are having a sleepover, when hearing dog Diesel is woken in the middle of the night by a strange hissing noise. Is there a slithery snake in the kitchen?

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Bactrian Camel and Lynx
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet Bactrian camels and a lynx.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Doug wants to be in the school show, so he asks Waffle to help him with his act. They practice ventriloquism in the kitchen together. Doug pretends to speak Waffles voice but Waffle doesnt really understand what to do, so Doug asks Simon for advice. Simon suggests Doug does something for the school show that hes good at already. Simon demonstrates what hes good at by singing Waffle Doggy.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona tries to keep Baba clean on a muddy Spring day.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The `Small Potatoes sing and dance their way across the stage in this show stopping musical number.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: TapTaps sister, TipTop, comes for a visit and amazes Yukee with her drumming skills. She is deaf, and she drums just as well as TapTap, making Yukee wonder how she does it. With TipTops help, Yukee learns how to feel the music.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When thunder and lightning scare the squirrels, Duggee teaches them about bravery.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Yakka Dee! celebrates children communicating - because talking fills you up with whizz, pop, bang! In this episode, Dee encourages a friend to say bus and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different buses - red, double-decker and bendy! We see Dee waiting for the bus and driving a bus in a picture on the wall.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Meet two new super-duper rectangles as they figure themselves out! Forty-Eight is 8 chunky Sixes and 6 chunky Eights, and teleporter Forty-two is 7 lots of Six and 6 lots of Seven.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: M cant resist munching everything in sight until a giant toad appears. Suddenly he is not the biggest eater around.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy ballet twirls with the Tiddlytubbies and the children in Tummy Tales put on a ballet show for their family at home.

programme.sub-title: Raa Raa and the Funniest Laugh
programme.desc: Raa Raa and his friends are looking for things that make funny noises, but Pia cant seem to find one that will make everyone laugh.

programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines, all of which are different kinds of motorbikes! Grace rides a classic touring bike, then shows off futuristic self-balancing bikes before heading to an indoor motocross course to ride an electric dirt bike.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When an underwater storm destroys a small reef, which a frogfish and other creatures depend on, Tweak and her crewmates must convert the Gup-F into an artificial reef before another storm strikes!

programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Dash in the Dark
programme.desc: When Peter and his friends go into the woods at night to catch a jar full of glow worms for Cotton-tails night light, they discover Cotton-tail isnt the only rabbit afraid of the dark.

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Zola
programme.desc: Zola, a Detroit waitress, is seduced into a weekend of stripping in Florida for some quick cash - but the trip becomes a sleepless 48-hour odyssey involving a nefarious friend, her pimp and her idiot boyfriend.

programme.title: Shiva Baby
programme.desc: While at a Jewish funeral service with her parents, a college student has an awkward encounter with her sugar daddy and her ex-girlfriend.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Ride Lonesome
programme.desc: A sheriff-turned-bounty hunter captures a notorious gunfighter, hoping to flush out his murderous brother and provoke a bloody shoot-out, but he finds several others are following the same prey.

programme.title: Yellow Sky
programme.desc: Seven outlaws rob a bank in Arizona then head for the badlands where they encounter an old man and his granddaughter, who have a hidden cache of gold. The outlaws make a deal to share it but some of them choose to cheat. Their leader, who has fallen for the granddaughter, takes on the double-crossers, finally facing one of them in a show-down.

programme.title: For Whom the Bell Tolls
programme.desc: Robert Jordan is the idealist who joins the Republicans and is charged with blowing up a strategically important bridge. Among the group aiding him are Maria, with who he falls in love, Pablo, the drunken leader of the group and his wife Pilar, who eventually takes charge.

programme.title: Monkey Business
programme.desc: A laboratory chimp inadvertently dumps an anti-ageing potion into a water cooler. An absent-minded professor and his wife drink the elixir and find themselves regressing to a state of childhood.

programme.title: Taxi
programme.desc: A bumbling policeman enlists the aid of a skilful taxi driver to chase Brazilian bank robbers through New York.

programme.title: The Equalizer
programme.desc: A former commando who had faked his own death comes out of retirement to rescue a young girl from violent Russian mobsters.

programme.title: Dejà Vu
programme.desc: Dejà vu is what unexpectedly guides agent Doug Carlin through an investigation into a crime. After an explosion in New Orleans, Doug is called to check and recover the evidence, but Carlins enquiries uncover a connection between the bomb accelerant and the body of a local girl named Claire Kuchever. Things become even stranger when Doug hears his own voice on Claires answering machine.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers roar back into life with a madcap tour of the Mississippi. They visit Memphis for the tastes and sounds of soul, meeting the queen of soul food.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The biker boys walk in the footsteps of music legends and experience some amazing Southern food. They fulfil a lifelong dream when they visit Graceland.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Easy Weeknight Dinners
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her Cajun Surf and Turf Pasta, Pineapple Chicken Stir-Fry. Shortcut Broccoli and Tomato Flatbread Pizzas; Street Corn Quesadilla. These speedy suppers are ideal for Monday to Friday meals that need to be ready in moments.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her Italian Pork Sandwiches and crowd-pleasing Butternut Squash and Black Bean Enchiladas; Slow-Cooker Teriyaki Ribs and a Greek 7-Layer Dip thats perfect for fall.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Farmhouse Roast Chicken and Creamy Prawn Linguine
programme.desc: Jamie gives traditional roast chicken a delicious French twist; shows us tasty ways to use up stale bread; and cooks his take on a creamy prawn linguine for his friend and mentor Gennaro Contaldo.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Roast Chicken Margherita and Prawn Toast Toastie
programme.desc: Jamie reinvents a roast chicken by combining it with the flavours of a margherita pizza, then whips up some prawn toast toasties, the perfect snack for sharing.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Roasted shrimp with feta; caramel ice cream; garden pasta.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Honey white bread; open tuna and hummus sandwich; mussels with basil.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In round five, everyone is out to avoid the dreaded soggy bottom as the seven remaining bakers face pies. Theyll begin with their signature family pie, before the dreaded technical challenge which sees lifelong vegetarian Jason delivering six beautifully baked and seasoned pork pies to the judging table. Finally, there is a sweet showstopper challenge in the shape of a meringue pie.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its an all-lady quarter-final as the remaining five have to impress with their dessert skills. In the signature bake, judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood are expecting to see and taste baked cheesecakes that reach their high expectations. The technical challenge is the bete noir of most bakers - a chocolate roulade. Finally, hundreds of choux pastry profiteroles must be perfected.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James visits a bakery made famous by `Sex and the City. Back in the kitchen, James whips up a strawberry gateaux and has some fun with cornflake cookies.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: Jamess cake adventures leads him to find out about two of New Yorks classic puddings, the New York cheesecake and Baked Alaska.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James heads to Brooklyn, where a local baker shows him around, before making some Whoopie Pies. He then meets a larger than life Brooklyn baking master.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James travels to Harlem to find a bakery in the heart of the community famous for its carrot cake, before moving on to a blueberry farm.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Chef Grudge Match
programme.desc: Laila invites four chefs to resolve fiery recipe rivalries. Mika Leon squares off against Brad Delgado, accusing him of stealing her recipes. Then, Tommy Jobst and Chris Lombardi settle who builds the absolute best burgers in the town of Davis, CA.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy tries all cosmopolitan comfort food. He samples corned beef and cabbage in Portsmouth, New Hampshire, authentic Ethiopian specialties in Los Angeles and over-the-top hot dogs in Minneapolis.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys picking up burgers to buns to bagels. In Portsmouth, NH, he visits a deli serving lamb burgers and ribs, and in Los Angeles, a Koreatown favourite.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy stops in small towns for big-time eats. In Raymond, NH, a butchery cranks out corn dogs from scratch. In Rocky Hill, NJ, a 1700s inn-turned-gastropub dishes out a tomato twist on burgers and disco fries.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys going coast-to-coast discovering righteous flavours in Oregon and New Hampshire. Check out an octopus dish and classic biscuits and gravy in Portland, Oregon, then head to Portsmouth, New Hampshire, for pork belly tacos.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Des Moines, Adam takes on the Adam Emmenecker Challenge at Jethros BBQ: 15 minutes to finish a mammoth sandwich.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Knoxville, Tennessee, Adam braves the Gigante Burrito Challenge at Sweet Ps BBQ.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: A client travels from South Carolina to order a giant flip-flop cake. Plus, a local school asks Buddy to create a kid-friendly cake to celebrate the continuation of their studies.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Pipes, a Phoenix, and a Problematic Kitchen
programme.desc: Buddy creates a phoenix cake for an Irish bar; not an easy task, since the ovens are being replaced. Later, Joey tests out the new ovens by making his famous Irish soda bread.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs into the traditions of many different cultures including a Mexican joint in Houston and a Jewish deli-on-wheels in Orlando, Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy heats up with spice and cools off with ice starting with hot dishes in California and Virginia before chilling with a Hawaiian tropical shaved ice.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a multi-generational joint bringing NOLA to LA, a unique Minneapolis deli serving up meatless `meats and a Hawaiian noodle spot dishing out ramen and Vietnamese crepes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Lunch with Ina is up for auction. The winning bidders can look forward to a delicious lunch of chilled cucumber soup with shrimp, lobster and shells salad, along with Eton Mess.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its Friday night and Ina is cooking up a delicious steak dinner for Jeffrey. This time Jeffreys making his own supper on the grill. Theres herbed ricotta bruschetta, grilled steak and Arugula.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is taking a trip down memory lane and updating classic recipes with a modern take. First up theres meatloaf, then shes updating an appetiser classic - smoked salmon devilled eggs.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Today is all about desserts in Inas hall of fame. She makes an incredible frozen mocha mousse, easy French apple galettes and balsamic strawberries.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is sharing her secrets on how to get professional results at home. Theres a Barefoot classic - lemon chicken breasts - and a recipe from one of her favourite New York restaurants.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: This is all about easy desserts. Theres a fruit filled cranberry and apple cake and a dessert master class from patisserie chef, Claudia Fleming, who shares her recipe for lemon cream napoleon.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to heart, baking treats to share with loved ones. She kicks things off with her epic chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Baking spectacular treats can be pricey, and after a strained 2020, many of us are looking for ways to tighten the purse strings. Nadiya believes you can create something delicious without breaking the bank or compromising on flavour.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite biscuits and bites to snack on at any time of the day. Nadiya believes that a homemade biscuit is one of lifes greatest pleasures, so she kicks off with her simple but extremely beautiful amaretti biscuits.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya takes things up a baking gear as she turns her attention to celebration bakes. Having won The Great British Bake Off and baked the Queens 90th birthday cake, Nadiya is a true expert when it comes to elaborate cakes to mark a special occasion.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: Jamess culinary tour takes him to the Camargue, where he learns all about the Camargue bull and the delicate hand-harvested salty crust fleur de sel.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James is in the city of Arles, famous for its Roman architecture and home to the largest market in Provence. James cooks a dish of John Dory using produce from the market.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: The Bikers cook dishes perfect for cosy suppers, comfort straight from the oven, some for sharing everyday, others for a special treat. Theres a new take on classic steak and chips, and a sticky gingerbread thats got to be the ultimate in comfort baking.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si and Dave cook dishes guaranteed to turn up the heat. They have a spicy new take on a family favourite with their roasted spiced cauliflower cheese, and they take inspiration from the Caribbean with a plate of jerk pork chops.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Dave enjoys a taste of his native county, Cumbria, as the Bikers cook recipes with great local ingredients. These include a comforting supper dish made from Cumberland sausage and the ultimate Cumbrian dessert, sticky toffee pudding.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers cook comfort food oozing with traditional values. Theres a twist on a classic pudding, a perfect teatime treat, and Si and Dave take two breakfast favourites to make one lip-smacking new dish.

programme.title: Secret Eats With Adam Richman
programme.desc: Adam Richman seeks out off-menu dishes in Reykjavik, Iceland, including a holiday-inspired pizza with unusual toppings, a meatball sandwich at a laundromat and traditional Icelandic delicacies hidden in a bus station.

programme.title: Secret Eats With Adam Richman
programme.desc: Adam Richman looks for hidden eats in Ilulissat and Oqaatsut, Greenland, including off-menu reindeer schnitzel, fried rice that combines Thai and Greenlandic flavours and an off-the-grid restaurant he must pass icebergs to find.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris grabbing creative flavour combos that pack a punch! First, an NYC couple is bringing Greek grub to Naples, FL. A Dallas spot is serving over-the-top pork belly lollipops, and a Triple-G winner is putting out next-level ramen in Chicago.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris diving into Kenyan curried goat and rocking Moroccan lamb in Tallahassee, FL. Plus, a Chicago spot is serving up big-time bechamel arancini, and a deli in Gainesville, FL, is bringing the heat with salt-and-pepper smoky sausages.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy hits the road looking for the best chicken dinners and finds a rocker mums favourite chicken, fried Thai wings and a California hot dog spot with unique chicken and waffles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy hits up three American cities for big international flavours. He visits a Filipino joint in LA, samples Ethiopian cuisine in San Francisco and bites into Indian classics in Minneapolis.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys getting ready for Turkey Day! In Princeton, New Jersey, Reverend Run jumps in for crepes, a New Hampshire cafe cooks up terrific turkey meatloaf and Guy finds the spirit of the season in Louisville at a `pay-what-you-can joint.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri is checking out fried bologna and veggie burgers in Louisville, KY, and brunch staples at a diner-restaurant mash up in Portland, Oregon.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys picking up burgers to buns to bagels. In Portsmouth, NH, he visits a deli serving lamb burgers and ribs, and in Los Angeles, a Koreatown favourite.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys going off the beaten path to check out funky finds. In North Carolina, an eatery tucked inside a beer store slings crazy waffle creations, and in Texas, a not-your-everyday fusion joint mashes up barbecue with sushi.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy heads to Cancun, Mexico, to dig into cuisine at places like an authentic, family-owned spot where Grandma is cooking up rellenos and killer chicharron gorditas. Then, get an inside look at Guys Playa del Carmen restaurant.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits an all-female lineup thats rockin the house in Houston and Orlando, Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs into the traditions of many different cultures including a Mexican joint in Houston and a Jewish deli-on-wheels in Orlando, Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy heats up with spice and cools off with ice starting with hot dishes in California and Virginia before chilling with a Hawaiian tropical shaved ice.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryans dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterdays results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mums Flu Fighting Chicken Soup. Jasons `A Tea From The Heart is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chriss `Everything But The Squeal is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jasons `For The Rich And The Poor showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the services founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. Hes cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local childrens hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric AandE in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Toms cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its time for the two remaining chefs representing the South West to go head to head as they cook their menus again for the exacting panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. With a spot in the National Finals at stake, both are aiming for perfection as they bid for glory. The judges are joined by Barbara Childs, a Matron in the Cardiac Intensive Care Unit at Great Ormond Street Hospital, who isnt afraid to speak her mind in the chamber.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwells in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all gunning for tens. Craig pays tribute to a friend who has revolutionised hospital food in Morecambe Bay, while Liam is inspired by health advice his grandma gave him and Ellis is dedicating his dish to the staff who delivered his son Albert. With cod, mackerel, shrimp and cockles on the menu, which of the chefs will score highly?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Time for the chefs to cook their mains and the pressure is on. Craig is hoping to impress using two cuts of Dexter beef, while Liam prepares his take on a Bajan lamb stew and Ellis prepares four cuts of suckling pig. Liam meets his personal hero, Nola Ishmael OBE, who became the UKs first ever black Director of Nursing.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the North West chefs to impress in a bid to make it through to the regional final. Liam is cooking `Not So Hot Toddy in honour of a friend who works nightshifts in the NHS, while Ellis `Worth Their Weight in Gold is dedicated to the staff who saved his brothers life and Craigs dish `The Missed Celebration pays tribute to his wife with an unusual Christmas concept.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the week return to cook their menus again. Judging them are Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver who are joined by Dr Jake Dunning MBE, an infectious disease specialist who has been recognised for his work during the West Africa Ebola epidemic.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs are doing battle to represent Northern Ireland. Tommy Heaney, Head Chef at the Great House Hotel in South Wales is taken on by Shauna Froydenlund, Chef Patron at Marcus Warings two Michelin starred restaurant and James Devine, the 2017 National Chef of the Year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course, the chefs return to the kitchen to cook their fish dishes. James is making his elevated take on a fried chicken takeaway as a tribute to the NHS staff who care for his sister. Shauna is also taking inspiration from a personal connection with a tribute to staff from a facility in her hometown of Londonderry. Tommy is hoping to impress with a dish dedicated to the discovery of DNA.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two courses down its time for the chefs to cook their main courses. Its the battle of beef with each using the same meat in their innovative creations. Tommy also meets the first baby ever born on the NHS.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert course and the final chance for the chefs to impress. After such a tightly fought battle all week, each is hoping to deliver a technically perfect dessert. Tommy and James are both using passion fruit in their complex dishes while Shauna is hoping to use her pastry chef training to score full marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs representing Northern Ireland go head to head. Judging as ever are Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined by Shehan Hettiaratchy, lead trauma surgeon at St Marys Hospital who helped treat victims of the Grenfell Tower fire.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs from all 8 regions must now battle it out for the honour of a spot on 2018s `Feast To Say Thank You banquet menu, celebrating 70 years of the NHS, one of Britains most prized institutions.Each day the regional champions will be cooking one of their courses again for the discerning panel of judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but after some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the starter round, everyone is desperate to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. Once again the chefs present their innovative, witty or heart warming creations, with lobster, mackerel, John Dory, turbot and hake among the dishes being scrutinized.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. Both chefs and judges alike are amazed at the high standard of cooking and an incredible number of 10s are dished out as the chefs serve up venison, rabbit, duck, beef, lamb and pork for scrutiny.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything theyve got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that theyll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale as the winning chefs cook their dishes at a banquet for the heroes of the NHS. Having arrived at St Bartholomews Hospital, the chefs are shown the incredible location for event, the almost three hundred year old Great Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central regions top chefs, returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett go into battle. They begin with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett fight it out for fish course glory. After plating up late last time will Aktar manage to be on time today?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central region chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poynton and Jason Hodnett go head to head cooking their main courses for the veteran judge. With Mark and Aktar fighting for the lead can Jason sneak under the radar to take them by surprise?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Central chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poyton and Jason Hodnett are all striving for one of two spots in the regional final. Will Jasons elaborate chocolate medal dessert see him triumph or sent home empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central region chefs cook all four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge Max Hastings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course and as the chefs arrive they discover theres added competition following the London and South East regions shock result. The judges are joined by D-Day veteran George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course and as the chefs arrive they discover theres added competition following the London and South East regions shock result. The judges are joined by D-Day veteran George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by Ken Sturdy who landed on Sword Beach on D-Day and who helped judge the North West region.

programme.title: Police Ten 7
programme.desc: An in depth and griping look at the day to day work of the police forces of New Zealand.

programme.title: Good News from GREAT!

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives
programme.desc: The Nazi regime lasted from 1933 to 1945 and was one of the most horrific periods of history. However, Adolf Hitler was not working alone. He had a circle of some of the most barbaric men alongside him, who helped make the atrocities possible.

programme.title: Air Wars
programme.desc: An in-depth look at the incredible story of the air raid planned by Lt Col Doolittle on the Japanese mainland in retaliation for Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: Air Wars

programme.title: Air Wars
programme.desc: An in-depth look at the history and tactics of the Kamikaze units and the logistical difficulties faced by the Japanese airforce during WWII.

programme.title: Wartime Crime
programme.desc: During the Nazi occupation of France, Dr Marcel Petiot embarked on a killing spree in which at least 23 victims died. Hear more about his heinous crimes.

programme.title: Wartime Crime
programme.desc: Hear how the American occupation of a German castle and a scandalous wartime affair lead to one of the most elaborate treasure heists of all time.

programme.title: Wartime Crime

programme.title: Wartime Crime

programme.title: Wartime Crime

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.desc: After the Dutch Republic declared its independence from Spain, it was cut off from the lucrative spice trades.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick and river-searching expert Beau head to Salisbury and explore the River Avon.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: This first episode takes Dan behind the battlements of Dover Castle, where he descends into an underground lair to discover how a vast network of tunnels has been used to defend both the castle and the country for centuries.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Built by William the Conqueror, the Tower of London is one of Britains most iconic landmarks. It has been a military fortress, a palace, a royal mint, a prison, a zoo and a place of execution, a silent witness to some of the most momentous events in our history. Dan Jones reveals some of its fascinating secrets and tells the stories of the traitors, rebels and royalty who met their end inside its imposing walls.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: Salvaging a yacht in Islamorda, Florida; 44-foot launch hung up on a dam on the Mississippi River.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: Jeremy Birch takes on a salvage to raise a vessel with a concrete hull thats been in the Fraser River for a month, adding 30 tons to its deadweight.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Norfolk, flint tools unearthed recently push the earliest human occupation back by 200,000 years, to around one million years ago. In Orkney an early farm yields glimpses of our ancestors earliest religious beliefs and customs.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick and river-searching expert Beau head to Salisbury and explore the River Avon.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The team find evidence from one of Scotlands bloodiest eras as they wade through the waters of the River Garry in the Scottish Highlands, the site of the first major clash between Highland Jacobites and the government army.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, Britains most Regency town, Professor Alice Roberts attends a period society ball, investigates social hierarchy and explores the Regency fondness for enemas.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Belfast, the UKs most Victorian city, Alice Roberts visits the shipyards that built the Titanic, gets locked up in the Crumlin Road gaol and learns about 19th-century etiquette.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes 45 miles through the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the areas rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At the impressively preserved Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps Tony relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. Tony finishes in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. On three occasions, Highland armies, assisted by the French and the Spanish, attempted to overthrow the King and put a Stuart back on the throne.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Marden Henge: The communal sweat lodges and feasting remains that illuminate the lost rituals of Stonehenge. Durotriges: A glimpse into the bizarre animal sacrifice rituals offered to their gods by a mysterious Celtic tribe of the first century BC.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the most exciting recent archaeological finds in the east of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy, and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Alien monoliths haunt the coast of England; an obliterated jungle points to a brutal narco gold rush; evidence of a strange blood-red creature emerging from the deep.

programme.desc: A satanic symbol that points to an army of the dead in Siberia; a massive stone giant in a remote desert; evidence of a Russian doomsday device right on Americas doorstep; cutting-edge technology helps experts answer sinister mysteries from space.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed

programme.desc: Theodores new talking teddy bear is driving Alvin and Simon nuts so they decide to rewire the bears hard drive so the bear will follow their directions.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave is working on an important new song for the music festival, but the Chipmunks accidentally ruin it. Can they fix it in time for the big performance?

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittany takes up painting to impress her fashion idol, but her idol only likes her art after Alvin ruins it.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore gets roped into doing Alvins babysitting job with a spoiled four year old. Kevin agrees to be Alvins assistant to get closer to the music industry, but ends up running errands instead.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Of Mice and Munks; Simon Saves the World
programme.desc: The Chipettes fall in love with a mouse thats doomed to be fed to a snake as part of a science class; Simons new invention threatens to destroy the world.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and The Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave writes a hit love song from a poem he found; since Simon wrote the poem, he panics his secret will come out.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittanys style icon proclaims Jeanette is a fashion trendsetter; Theodore thinks that he is a real superhero.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin catches Simon taking a test to discover his soul mate. Simon and Alvin decide to rebel and launch their own campaigns for class president.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin helps the mathletes stand up to the school bullies, but in the process they become bullies themselves; Alvin befriends a new student, but Theodore learns something surprising about him.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin is convinced that Theodore has cursed him to never lie again and cant help himself from blabbing the truth.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Miss Croner finds termites have infested her home, she moves in with the chipmunks and drives everyone crazy.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Theos Big Night Out; The Bodyguard
programme.desc: Theodore tries to overcome his fear of sleepovers by spending the night at Kevins house without Alvin or Simon. Plus, when Alvin sees every celebrity with their own personal bodyguard, he decides to train Theodore for the job.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Animal House Party; Dr. Zap vs. Electroboy
programme.desc: When Derek overhears that Alvin and his brothers are alone for the weekend, he plans a wild party at their house. Theodore is hit by lightning and believes hes like his comic book hero, until hes told hes actually a villain.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Simon falls ill, Alvin takes his place on a camping trip only to discover its actually an archaeological dig; the Chipmunks dont know what to do when they suspect Dave is lying about the various awards hes won.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin believes his mojo was in a cap he regrets giving to a fan. During a visit to the zoo, Alvin is mistaken for a Tasmanian devil.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Simon creates a Super Hero costume for a convention hes attending. But when Alvin wants to join, the two fight over who the real hero is. When Alvins bicycle is stolen, he joins forces with Officer Dangus to solve the case.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore is stuck taking care of Miss Croners cats during a week that Dave wants the boys to focus only on school. The Chipettes challenge the Chipmunks to go three hours without their favourite things.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittanys fear of bees begins to turn into admiration when shes shrunk down to their size and has to experience life as a bee.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Simon invents a robot with emotions and an ability to learn which backfires when hes exposed to the internet.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: You Troll; Life of the Party
programme.desc: The Chipmunks try to keep Dave from getting on the Internet after a performance he posts gets negative comments. The Chipmunks along with Kevin and Cheesy decide to crash BoCarters birthday yacht party after not being invited.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: A single father experiences the trials and tribulations of raising a group of young chipmunks who are also famous rock stars touring the world with their best friends, the Chipettes.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave finds himself in school with Alvin after he discovers that he never graduated from high school; Simons new friend decides to ditch him to spend time with Alvin.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin encourages Simon to write an email, venting about his professor. When the email accidentally gets sent, they must stop the teacher from reading it. When Simon notices that Theodore has a hard time saying no, he creates the No-Buddy.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore gives Dave an ugly handmade vest for his birthday and insists he wear it to an upcoming business meeting. When Theodore finds a winning lottery ticket, everyone has their own ideas on how the money should be spent.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Simon Says; Sing Like a Canary
programme.desc: When Alvin accuses Simon of being too safe, Simon agrees to do whatever crazy thing Alvin wants him to do. A cute singing bird replaces Alvin in the band after Alvin makes too many demands as a performer.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After getting detention again, Alvins convinced Dave will drive him to the desert and abandon him there; Alvin and Theodore play a seemingly innocent game, which leads to unexpected consequences.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore is passed over when Alvin and Eleanor train as lifeguards; Miss Croners cherries are lost.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Desperate to avenge last years humiliating loss, the chipmunks argue over who is best suited to lead them to victory; Alvin grows tired of seeing his celebrity status overshadowed by reality TV stars, so he decides to become one himself.

programme.sub-title: Were the Chipmunks; Save the Dance

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When the boys get stuck with Miss Miller as a baby sitter, they each take a crack at finding a replacement. Alvin volunteers to caddy a charity golf tournament and surprisingly gets paired up with Miss Smith.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The Chipmunks and Chipettes compete in a dodgeball tournament but no-one expects Theo to be their secret weapon. The Chipmunks find a shack that magically fills with toys and games, but is it magic or something more sinister.

programme.desc: When Theodores teddy bear, Talking Teddy, gets bear-napped, the boys work with Officer Dangus to get him back. When Alvin starts stealing the morning paper to sell at school, Dave blames the paperboy and refuses to pay up.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Animal House Party; Dr. Zap vs. Electroboy

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Simon falls ill, Alvin takes his place on a camping trip only to discover its actually an archaeological dig; the Chipmunks dont know what to do when they suspect Dave is lying about the various awards hes won.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin believes his mojo was in a cap he regrets giving to a fan. During a visit to the zoo, Alvin is mistaken for a Tasmanian devil.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks

programme.title: Johnny Cash: Music in Review
programme.desc: The film features rare archive interviews with Johnny and the reflections of a team of leading critics and working musicians, including Cowboy Jack Clement and Bob Wooton and Fluke Holland from The Tennessee Three. This penetrating critical analysis draws on rare archive footage of Johnny Cash in performance, from radio and television archives around the world tracing the career of the man dubbed the man in black.

programme.title: Paul McCartney: A Musicares Tribute
programme.desc: Paul McCartney headlines a star-studded concert, which features the likes Foo Fighters, Coldplay, Katy Perry and Neil Young, who each give their unique takes on some Beatles classics.

programme.title: Of Monsters and Men - Main Square Festival 2013
programme.desc: Icelandic band Of Monsters and Men performs a set in sunny Arras, France.

programme.title: Enrique Iglesias - Live from the Odyssey Arena
programme.desc: Filmed at Odyssey Arena, Belfast, this concert film will give all fans a spectacular Live experience from start to finish. Classic Enrique Iglesias featuring unforgettable versions of his hits: Bailamos, Hero, Be With You and many more...

programme.title: Milky Chance - Live Garorock Festival 2017
programme.desc: The German duet is back and better than ever! Milky Chance is the success story of two school friends, Clemens Rehbein and Philipp Dausch. After a long break, 2017 marks the release of a second, long-waited, album Blossom.

programme.title: Interpol - Rock En Seine
programme.desc: Interpol is an American rock band from Manhattan, New York,[2] formed in 1997. Their original line-up consisted of Paul Banks, Daniel Kessler, Carlos Dengler and Greg Drudy.

programme.title: Jamie Cullum: Jazz a? Vienne 2017
programme.desc: Watch the performance of Jamie Cullum live at Jazz à Vienne, in 2017.

programme.title: Depeche Mode - Rock Am Ring
programme.desc: Rock Am Ring (Nurburgring), Rock Im Park (Nuremberg) is a music festival that takes place annually at the Nurburgring Race Track in Western Germany. Featuring Depeche Mode.

programme.title: The Black Keys - Live at Main Square Festival
programme.desc: Main Square Festival is a music festival that takes place in Arras, France, lasting three days.

programme.title: Oasis: Live at Wembley Arena 2008
programme.desc: Filmed at Wembley Arena, London, this concert film gives all fans a spectacular live experience from start to finish.

programme.title: Guns N Roses: Live From The O2 Arena London
programme.desc: Watching this mega-production of Guns N Roses performing live at the O2 Arena to its fans singing and vibrating to each song including Paradise City, Sweet Child O Mine, November Rain and many more.

programme.title: Metallica - Orion Festival: Tour Through the Never
programme.desc: Metallica performs their top tracks, including "Master of Puppets and One", "Sad But True", and "Seek and Destroy", at the Orion Festival in Atlantic City.

programme.title: Jimi Hendrix - Electric Church: Atlanta Pop Festival
programme.desc: Electric Church presents Jimi Hendrix in full flight at the 1970 Atlanta Pop Festival before the largest U.S. audience of his career.

programme.title: Queens of the Stone Age - Zenith Paris 2013
programme.desc: Get your fill of riff-oriented heavy rock with this dark and moody performance by Queens of the Stone Age. Best enjoyed with the volume turned up high!

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike tracks down a rare 91 Alfa Romeo 164L. He loves the look, but Ant has his work cut out for him. The car has perfect bodywork, but it has a leaking engine.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer and Ant Anstead work together to give new life to run-down classic automobiles to resell them at a profit. Working on a tight budget, Mike must find a vehicle for Ant to fix, but that isnt always the easiest task.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.sub-title: I Deserve to Live, They Dont
programme.desc: Millionaire CEO Kim Bailey suspects her employees are stealing from her. When a colleague vanishes, an FBI investigation leads to a deadly conclusion.

programme.title: American Monster: Abuse of Power

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Judge Robert Rinder examines the story of a volatile relationship that ended with horrifying results, and a devastating crime committed on a 15-week-old baby.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates

programme.title: The Friday the 13th Murders
programme.desc: A young woman vanishes after entering a patrol car; for 35 years, shes a Jane Doe; as fear and rumours consume a small town, a chilling clue under hypnosis cracks the case open-and exposes a predator no one saw coming.

programme.title: American Monster

programme.title: American Monster

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.desc: A rumour swirls around the bordertown of Brownsville, Texas, that Kimberly Avila may have been kidnapped or murdered by a member of the cartel when she goes missing on May 13, 2017.

programme.desc: Successful lawyer and new mother, Ciera Breland disappears after she and her husband visit his mother in Georgia. It then emerges that actually her perfect life isnt what it seems.

programme.sub-title: The Sweetest Man in the World

programme.title: Disappeared

programme.desc: Cindy Monkman turns heads wherever she goes, but by age 30, shes looking for something serious. Michael Apelt, a German businessman, seems to be the answer to her prayers, until their first Christmas together turns into a horror story.

programme.title: American Monster

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents

programme.desc: After the triumph of the machines on Judgement Day and with killer robots hunting down the last vestiges of humanity, resistance fighter John Connor battles back. However, the arrival of a mysterious stranger threatens to tip the balance in favour of the machines.

programme.title: xXx: The Return of Xander Cage
programme.desc: After coming out of self-imposed exile, daredevil operative Xander Cage must race against time to recover a sinister weapon known as Pandoras Box, a device that controls every military satellite in the world. Recruiting a new group of thrill-seeking cohorts, Xander finds himself entangled in a deadly conspiracy that points to collusion at the highest levels of government.

programme.title: The Beekeeper
programme.desc: One mans brutal campaign for vengeance takes on national stakes after its revealed hes a former operative of a powerful and clandestine organisation known as Beekeepers.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: Adaptation of the adventures of the swinging comic-strip hero. programme.title: Clash of the Titans

programme.desc: The comic book web-slinger returns for a blockbusting sequel. Crime-fighting has cost Peter Parker his best friend and his true love Mary Jane. However, just as he quits as Spider-Man, New York comes under threat from Dr Octopus, a metal-tentacled madman. Peter has no choice but to swing back into action.

programme.title: Fast and Furious 8
programme.desc: The globetrotting crew faces an unexpected challenge when a mysterious woman named Cipher forces Dom to betray them all. Now, they must unite to bring home the man who made them a family and stop her from unleashing chaos on a grand scale.

programme.title: Terminator Salvation

programme.title: Wrath of the Titans

programme.title: Fox and Hare Save the Forest
programme.desc: One day, Fox and Hare discover that Owl has disappeared. They go looking for him in the forest with all their friends and discover a big lake that wasnt there before. It is the beginning of an exciting and funny adventure.
